title: 7 Key Moments from the 2026 Academy Awards
text: The 98th Academy Awards ceremony ran 3 hours and 40 minutes, featuring several notable achievements and memorable moments. Paul Thomas Anderson achieved his long-awaited Oscar success, winning Best Adapted Screenplay, Best Director, and Best Picture for "One Battle After Another." After 14 previous nominations, Anderson stated: "You make a guy work hard for this." Michael B. Jordan won Best Actor for "Sinners," while Jessie Buckley claimed Best Actress for "Hamnet," becoming the first Irish performer to win in that category. Cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw became the first woman and Black person to win the cinematography award. Amy Madigan won Best Supporting Actress for "Weapons," ending a 40-year wait between her first nomination and her first win—a record for the category. Host Conan O'Brien delivered varied comedy, from sharp political commentary to heartfelt observations about global cinema collaboration.
